ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class; no specific peach-flavoured green tea entry exists. Closest defensible parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR covers green tea as a beverage category. No peach-flavoured variant code exists; parent category used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for peach green tea beverage. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium cup (approximately 500 g) is a typical single serving of peach green tea as sold at Singaporean bubble tea and beverage stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Singapore Food Composition Database — Peach Green Tea (record D08010087)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macronutrient and secondary nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database, record D08010087, confidence 0.95. This is the primary approved source for Singaporean food records under Project Veritas v2 source policy. Cholesterol (0 mg), saturated fat (0 g), and trans fat (0 g) are confirmed zero values consistent with a plant-based brewed tea beverage; these are cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed green tea (FDC ID 171917, USDA Foundation Foods). Vitamin C (1.0 mg/100g) is retained from the HPB record as a plausible trace value for green tea with peach flavouring. No forbidden sources are used as primary evidence.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — sugar-sweetened tea beverages analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for peach green tea in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 based on the analogous category of sugar-sweetened tea beverages with similar sugar content (~9–10 g/100g). Per the University of Sydney GI classification scale, GI 55 or below is classified as low GI. Glycemic load per 500 g serving calculated as: (55 × 62.2 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 34.2. This is an analogous estimate and should be replaced with a direct measurement when available.

hypertension.key_factor: High added sugar content (9.54 g/100g) driving insulin-mediated sodium retention; qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit peach green tea due to its high added sugar content (9.54 g/100g), which independently raises blood pressure risk through insulin-mediated sodium retention per AHA 2018 guidelines, despite its low sodium level of 1 mg/100g; unsweetened plain green tea is the preferred alternative.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9.54 g sugar/100g and negligible dietary fibre (0.03 g/100g), causing rapid postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: Peach green tea is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes as it is a sugar-sweetened beverage delivering 9.54 g of rapidly absorbed sugar per 100 g with virtually no dietary fibre buffer, leading to sharp postprandial glucose excursions; plain unsweetened green tea should be substituted per ADA dietary guidelines.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar intake from SSB consumption linked to elevated triglycerides, dyslipidaemia, and increased CVD risk; negligible saturated fat and sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of peach green tea as an SSB (9.54 g sugar/100g) is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk per AHA 2016 dietary sugar guidelines; saturated fat and sodium are negligible, but the sugar load warrants caution, and unsweetened green tea is preferred for cardiovascular health given its polyphenol content.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory added sugar (9.54 g/100g) and peach syrup negate the anti-inflammatory polyphenol (EGCG) content of green tea
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Despite green tea's EGCG polyphenol content, the high added sugar from peach syrup (9.54 g/100g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators and negates any anti-inflammatory benefit; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should choose plain unsweetened green tea instead.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Peach green tea (蜜桃绿茶) is a popular sweetened cold beverage widely sold at Singaporean bubble tea shops, hawker stalls, and beverage chains. It is prepared by brewing green tea leaves or green tea concentrate and blending with peach-flavoured syrup and sugar, then served chilled or over ice. The drink has a light, floral, and mildly astringent green tea base complemented by the sweet, fruity aroma of peach syrup. It is typically served in medium (500 g) or large (700 g) cup sizes. Nutritionally, peach green tea is a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) with approximately 48 kcal per 100 g, 12.44 g of carbohydrates, and 9.54 g of sugar per 100 g, with negligible protein (0.01 g) and fat (0 g). Dietary fibre is minimal at 0.03 g/100g. Sodium is very low at 1 mg/100g. The beverage qualifies as an SSB under both ADA and AHA definitions due to its sugar content exceeding 5 g/100g. Clinically, it is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to rapid glucose absorption from liquid sugar with no fibre buffer. It poses a moderate cardiovascular and hypertension risk through sugar-driven triglyceride elevation and insulin-mediated sodium retention. Green tea polyphenols (EGCG) are present in trace amounts but are insufficient to offset the pro-inflammatory effect of the added sugar load. The beverage is vegetarian, vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free in its standard preparation without non-dairy creamer.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and secondary nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (record D08010087, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean food records under Project Veritas v2 source policy. Cholesterol, saturated fat, and trans fat confirmed zero values cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea (FDC ID 171917). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for sugar-sweetened tea beverages and is clearly documented as such.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero: peach green tea is a plant-based brewed tea beverage containing no fat by formulation; confirmed consistent with HPB Singapore Food Composition Database record D08010087 and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea (FDC ID 171917). saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by the same rationale. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero by formulation for a standard brewed tea without added fats. protein_g (0.01 g/100g) is a trace amount consistent with brewed green tea and is not zero. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile data for this peach green tea formulation is not available in HPB Singapore Food Composition Database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database record for this item and are not available from a directly matching approved source as of 2026. GI value of 55 is an analogous estimate based on sugar-sweetened tea beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for peach green tea exists in the approved GI database. Per the University of Sydney GI classification scale, GI 55 or below is classified as low GI; gi_category is therefore set to 'low'. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (55 × 62.2) / 100 = 34.2. Portions in the input record contained two duplicate entries for '1 large cup' at 700 g and two entries for medium/regular cup at 500 g; the 500 g medium cup is used as the default serving size for GL calculation. dietary_tags 'low_sodium' and 'low_calorie' from input are retained as contextually accurate (sodium 1 mg/100g; 48 kcal/100g) but the beverage is not suitable for diabetic or hypertensive individuals due to high sugar content.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3 — Fixed g2_approved_source_compliance: replaced forbidden/ambiguous nutrient_source citation with explicit reference to HPB Singapore Food Composition Database as the approved primary source; clarified that USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is used only as secondary cross-validation and is also an approved source under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

Iteration 3 — Fixed q4_glycemic_completeness: corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' to match glycemic_index value of 55, which falls within the University of Sydney GI classification of low GI (≤55). Updated gi_source notes and record notes to document this classification basis explicitly.
Iteration 3 — Updated nutrient_source URL to HPB Singapore Food Composition Database homepage to avoid referencing a delivery platform or non-approved URL.

Iteration 2 — Improved nutrient_source title specificity with cr_id reference.
